heading: Unsubsidized Stafford loans for middle-income borrowers
label: 1078–8 Unsubsidized Stafford loans for middle-income borrowers

(h). , inserted at end “Effective for loans for which the date of guarantee of principal is on or after , in lieu of the insurance premium authorized under the preceding sentence, each State or nonprofit private institution or organization having an agreement with the Secretary under shall collect and deposit into the Federal Student Loan Reserve Fund under , a Federal default fee of an amount equal to 1.0 percent of the principal amount of the loan, which fee shall be collected either by deduction from the proceeds of the loan or by payment from other non-Federal sources. The Federal default fee shall not be used for incentive payments to lenders.”Pub. L. 109–171, § 8014(b)(2)section 1078(b)(1) of this titlesection 1072a of this titleJuly 1, 20061998—Subsec. (b). , amended heading and text of subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Any student meeting the requirements for student eligibility under (including graduate and professional students as defined in regulations promulgated by the Secretary) shall be entitled to borrow an unsubsidized Stafford loan. Such student shall provide to the lender a statement from the eligible institution at which the student has been accepted for enrollment, or at which the student is in attendance, which—Pub. L. 105–244, § 423(a)section 1091 of this title“(1) sets forth such student’s estimated cost of attendance (as determined under section 1087 of this title);ll“(2) sets forth such student’s estimated financial assistance, including a loan which qualifies for subsidy payments under ; andsection 1078 of this title“(3) certifies the eligibility of the student to receive a loan under this section and the amount of the loan for which such student is eligible, in accordance with subsection (c) of this section.”Subsec. (d)(2). , in introductory provisions, inserted “(as defined in )” after “academic year” and struck out “or in any period of 7 consecutive months, whichever is longer,” after “or its equivalent”.Pub. (ii) and (iii) which read as follows:Pub. L. 105–244, § 423(b)(1)(B)section 1088 of this title“(ii) $2,500, if such student is enrolled in a program whose length is less than one academic year, but at least ⅔ of such an academic year; and“(iii) $1,500, if such student is enrolled in a program whose length is less than ⅔, but at least ⅓, of such an academic year;”.Subsec. (d)(2)(D). , (D), added subpar. (D).Pub. L. 105–244, § 423(b)(1)(C)Subsec. (d)(3). , inserted at end “Interest capitalized shall not be deemed to exceed such maximum aggregate amount.”Pub. L. 105–244, § 423(b)(2)Subsec. (e)(2). , amended heading and text of par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Interest on loans made under this section for which payments of principal are not required during the in-school and grace periods or for which payments are deferred under sections 1077(a)(2)(C) and 1078(b)(1)(M) of this title shall, if agreed upon by the borrower and the lender (A) be paid monthly or quarterly, or (B) be added to the principal amount of the loan not more frequently than quarterly by the lender. Such capitalization of interest shall not be deemed to exceed the annual insurable limit on account of the student.”Pub. L. 105–244, § 423(c)Subsec. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: It is the purpose of this section to authorize insured loans under this part that are first disbursed before , for borrowers who do not qualify for Federal interest subsidy payments under . Except as provided in this section, all terms and conditions for Federal Stafford loans established under shall apply to loans made pursuant to this section.July 1, 2010section 1078 of this titlesection 1078 of this title

determined and documented the student’s need for the loan based on the student’s estimated cost of attendance (as determined under section 1087 of this title) and the student’s estimated financial assistance, including a loan which qualifies for interest subsidy payments under ; andllsection 1078 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: certifying the eligibility of the student to receive a loan under this section and the amount of the loan for which such student is eligible, in accordance with subsection (c); and

children.label: B
children.text: setting forth a schedule for disbursement of the proceeds of the loan in installments, consistent with the requirements of .section 1078–7 of this title

children.heading: Determination of amount of loan
children.label: c Determination of amount of loan
children.text: The determination of the amount of a loan by an eligible institution under subsection (b) shall be calculated by subtracting from the estimated cost of attendance at the eligible institution any estimated financial assistance reasonably available to such student. An eligible institution may not, in carrying out the provisions of subsection (b) of this section, provide a statement which certifies the eligibility of any student to receive any loan under this section in excess of the amount calculated under the preceding sentence.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraphs (2), (3), and (4), the annual and aggregate limits for loans under this section shall be the same as those established under , less any amount received by such student pursuant to the subsidized loan program established under .section 1078(b)(1) of this titlesection 1078 of this title

children.heading: Limits for graduate, professional, and independent postbaccalaureate students
children.label: 2 Limits for graduate, professional, and independent postbaccalaureate students
i
children.text: in the case of such a student who is a graduate or professional student attending an eligible institution, $12,000; and

children.label: I
children.text: $7,000 for coursework necessary for enrollment in a graduate or professional program; and

children.label: II
children.text: $7,000 for coursework necessary for a professional credential or certification from a State required for employment as a teacher in an elementary or secondary school,

children.heading: Aggregate limit
children.label: B Aggregate limit
children.text: The maximum aggregate amount of loans under this section a student described in subparagraph (A) may borrow shall be the amount described in paragraph (1), adjusted to reflect the increased annual limits described in subparagraph (A), as prescribed by the Secretary by regulation.

children.heading: Annual limits
children.label: A Annual limits
children.text: The maximum annual amount of loans under this section an undergraduate dependent student (except an undergraduate dependent student whose parents are unable to borrow under or the Federal Direct PLUS Loan Program) may borrow in any academic year (as defined in ) or its equivalent shall be the sum of the amount determined under paragraph (1), plus $2,000.section 1078–2 of this titlesection 1088(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Aggregate limits
children.label: B Aggregate limits
children.text: The maximum aggregate amount of loans under this section a student described in subparagraph (A) may borrow shall be $31,000.

children.label: I
children.text: $6,000, if such student is enrolled in a program whose length is at least one academic year in length; or

children.label: II
children.text: if such student is enrolled in a program of undergraduate education which is less than one academic year, the maximum annual loan amount that such student may receive may not exceed the amount that bears the same ratio to the amount specified in subclause (I) as the length of such program measured in semester, trimester, quarter, or clock hours bears to one academic year;

children.label: II
children.text: if such student is enrolled in a program of undergraduate education, the remainder of which is less than one academic year, the maximum annual loan amount that such student may receive may not exceed the amount that bears the same ratio to the amount specified in subclause (I) as such remainder measured in semester, trimester, quarter, or clock hours bears to one academic year; and

children.label: I
children.text: , $6,000; orsection 1091(b)(3)(B) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: , $7,000.section 1091(b)(4)(B) of this title

children.heading: Aggregate limits
children.label: B Aggregate limits
children.text: The maximum aggregate amount of loans under this section a student described in subparagraph (A) may borrow shall be $57,500.

children.heading: Capitalized interest
children.label: 5 Capitalized interest
children.text: Interest capitalized shall not be deemed to exceed a maximum aggregate amount determined under subparagraph (B) of paragraph (2), (3), or (4).

children.heading: Payment of principal and interest
children.label: e Payment of principal and interest

children.heading: Commencement of repayment
children.label: 1 Commencement of repayment
children.text: Repayment of principal on loans made under this section shall begin at the beginning of the repayment period described in . Not less than 30 days prior to the anticipated commencement of such repayment period, the holder of such loan shall provide notice to the borrower that interest will accrue before repayment begins and of the borrower’s option to begin loan repayment at an earlier date.section 1078(b)(7) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: be paid monthly or quarterly; or

children.label: I
children.text: when the loan enters repayment;

children.label: II
children.text: at the expiration of a grace period, in the case of a loan that qualifies for a grace period;

children.label: III
children.text: at the expiration of a period of deferment or forbearance; or

children.label: IV
children.text: when the borrower defaults.

children.label: B
children.text: The capitalization of interest described in subparagraph (A) shall not be deemed to exceed the annual insurable limit on account of the student.

children.label: C
children.text: Interest shall not accrue on a loan deferred under section 1078(b)(1)(M)(v) or 1077(a)(2)(C)(iv) of this title.

children.heading: Subsidies prohibited
children.label: 3 Subsidies prohibited
children.text: No payments to reduce interest costs shall be paid pursuant to on loans made pursuant to this section.section 1078(a) of this title

children.heading: Applicable rates of interest
children.label: 4 Applicable rates of interest
children.text: Interest on loans made pursuant to this section shall be at the applicable rate of interest provided in .section 1077a of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of the periodic payment will be adjusted annually; or

children.label: B
children.text: the period of repayment of principal will be lengthened or shortened,

children.heading: Repayment period
children.label: 6 Repayment period
children.text: For purposes of calculating the repayment period under , such period shall commence at the time the first payment of principal is due from the borrower.section 1078(b)(9) of this title

children.heading: Qualification for forbearance
children.label: 7 Qualification for forbearance
children.text: A lender may grant the borrower of a loan under this section a forbearance for a period not to exceed 60 days if the lender reasonably determines that such a forbearance from collection activity is warranted following a borrower’s request for forbearance, deferment, or a change in repayment plan, or a request to consolidate loans in order to collect or process appropriate supporting documentation related to the request. During any such period, interest on the loan shall accrue but not be capitalized.

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 105–244, title IV, § 423(f)112 Stat. 1698Oct. 7, 1998
children.label: f Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 105–244, title IV, § 423(f)112 Stat. 1698Oct. 7, 1998

children.heading: Single application form and loan repayment schedule
children.label: g Single application form and loan repayment schedule
children.text: A guaranty agency shall use a single application form and a single repayment schedule for subsidized Federal Stafford loans made pursuant to and for unsubsidized Federal Stafford loans made pursuant to this section.section 1078 of this title

children.heading: Insurance premium
children.label: h Insurance premium
children.text: Each State or nonprofit private institution or organization having an agreement with the Secretary under may charge a borrower under this section an insurance premium equal to not more than 1.0 percent of the principal amount of the loan, if such premium will not be used for incentive payments to lenders. Effective for loans for which the date of guarantee of principal is on or after , and that are first disbursed before , in lieu of the insurance premium authorized under the preceding sentence, each State or nonprofit private institution or organization having an agreement with the Secretary under shall collect and deposit into the Federal Student Loan Reserve Fund under , a Federal default fee of an amount equal to 1.0 percent of the principal amount of the loan, which fee shall be collected either by deduction from the proceeds of the loan or by payment from other non-Federal sources. The Federal default fee shall not be used for incentive payments to lenders.section 1078(b)(1) of this titlesection 1078(b)(1) of this titlesection 1072a of this titleJuly 1, 2006July 1, 2010
